title: [InstCombine] Improve `foldAndOrOfICmpsUsingRanges`
summary:
The developers generalized an optimization by extending `foldAndOrOfICmpsUsingRanges` to handle masked comparisons. Originally, the function only combined ICmps comparing the same value directly. The patch adds support for patterns where two ICmps compare the same value after applying different masks (`(A & B) == C` and `(A & D) == E`).

Key generalization steps:
1. Added a second matching path using `getMaskedTypeForICmpPair` to detect masked comparison patterns
2. Unified handling by ensuring both paths set `V1 == V2` for the common base value
3. Moved the range-based folding earlier in `foldAndOrOfICmps` to catch more cases before other transforms

This approach transforms the specific `icmp_ne_sext_eq` pattern into a general mask-aware range analysis, enabling optimization of the `isFloat` test case and similar patterns. The strategy demonstrates how to extend existing infrastructure by adding new pattern recognition while reusing core analysis logic.
